summary: Iteration 2 testing complete: Backend 100% pass (20/20 new tests + 18/18 regression tests). Frontend: Found and fixed critical bug where login/register didn't call refresh() to get plan/limits/usage. After fix, all features working: usage banner, paywall screen, admin reports, privacy badges (APPROX). One minor issue: paywall navigation from usage banner needs longer wait time.

integration_issues.issue: FIXED: login() and register() in auth.tsx were setting user from API response but not calling refresh() to get full user object with plan, limits, and usage fields. This caused usage banner to not appear for free users.
integration_issues.fix_applied: Changed auth.tsx lines 62-71 to call await refresh() instead of setUser(data.user) after login/register

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all features working after auth.tsx fix, minor paywall navigation timing issue)

seed_data_creation: Backend tests created TEST_ prefixed data: fresh users, collections, private spots, saves, reports. All test data can be cleaned up by filtering TEST_ prefix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 2 complete and fully functional. Critical bug fixed: auth.tsx login/register now call refresh() to populate plan/limits/usage. All backend feature gating working (402 responses). All frontend UI features working: usage banner, paywall, admin reports, privacy badges. Minor note: sophie@photoscout.app was upgraded to 'pro' plan during testing, use test_free_user@test.com (password: test123) for testing free plan features.
rca_of_issue: Root Cause: The login() and register() functions in /app/frontend/src/auth.tsx were calling setUser(data.user) with the response from POST /api/auth/login or POST /api/auth/register. However, these endpoints only return basic user data with the 'plan' field but NOT the 'limits' and 'usage' fields. Only GET /api/auth/me computes and returns limits and usage dynamically. The usage banner in saved.tsx has a condition {user.plan === 'free' && user.limits && user.usage && ...} which requires all three fields. Without limits and usage, the banner didn't render. Fix: Changed login() and register() to call await refresh() instead of setUser(data.user). The refresh() function calls GET /api/auth/me which returns the full user object with plan, limits, and usage. After this fix, the usage banner displays correctly for free users.
